diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysuria is the cardinal symptom of lower-tract infection.
Dysuria also occurs in urethritis, especially with a post-coital onset.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary frequency and urgency with small voided volumes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequency/urgency with small volumes reflects bladder mucosal irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic discomfort and sense of incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic pain localises inflammation to the bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cloudy, foul-smelling urine with visible (macroscopic) haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory haemorrhagic cystitis produces visible blood and turbid, malodorous urine; patient-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
Visible haematuria is a bladder-cancer red flag if it persists after infection is treated.
Coitus mechanically introduces periurethral flora into the bladder — a recognised trigger of post-coital cystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors or systemic toxicity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic features are required for pyelonephritis/urosepsis; their absence keeps infection lower-tract.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of flank pain / costovertebral-angle tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CVA tenderness is elicited on examination; its documented absence localises infection to the lower tract. Clinician-obtained — sealed; the AI can only ask about loin pain, not elicit the sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild localised suprapubic tenderness on deep palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic tenderness supports bladder inflammation; requires an examining clinician's hands and cannot be obtained via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urine dipstick strongly positive for leukocyte esterase and nitrites, with blood

investigation_details.expected_result: Strongly positive leukocyte esterase and nitrites; significant blood present
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urinalysis the diagnosis and the exclusion of alternative causes of haematuria remain unconfirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive nitrites and leukocyte esterase strongly support bacterial UTI; requires a physical dipstick the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Formal midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities

investigation_details.investigation_name: Midstream urine (MSU) microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_details.expected_result: Significant bacteriuria with an identified uropathogen and antibiotic sensitivities
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific organism and sensitivities go unconfirmed, and empirical therapy cannot be tailored — particularly important with haematuria and recurrence risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Culture-confirmed significant bacteriuria is the reference standard for UTI; a laboratory test not available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dysuria + frequency/urgency + suprapubic pain + cloudy haematuric urine together give a high probability of lower UTI in a non-pregnant woman.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick and midstream urine (MSU) for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line confirmation of UTI; MSU indicated here given visible haematuria to identify the organism and confirm clearance.
Establishes pregnancy status in a reproductive-age woman, which reclassifies the UTI as complicated and changes safe antibiotic choice.
Not indicated for a first uncomplicated cystitis; reserved for recurrent infection, suspected obstruction, or persistent haematuria after treatment.
